Our main theoretical result is to show that more flexible posteriors necessarily yield better estimates of Pareto fronts. For instance, a Pareto front estimate obtained by merging full-Gaussian posteriors is expected to be better than that obtained by using isotropic Gaussian posteriors. We validate the theory through extensive empirical results on vision and language transformers where better Gaussian families consistently yields better or comparable Pareto fronts. Our work is a rare instance where Bayesian ideas are used to improve Pareto analysis.

light_analysis.overview: 提出变分模型合并方法，通过贝叶斯后验合并改进多任务微调中Pareto前沿的估计质量。
light_analysis.motivation: 多任务微调中，Pareto前沿有助于找到任务混合策略，但计算成本高昂。现有方法使用模型合并训练代理模型估计Pareto前沿，但未设计专门方法直接提升前沿质量。本文旨在填补这一空白，利用贝叶斯后验合并理论，通过更灵活的后验分布来获得更好的Pareto前沿估计。
light_analysis.method: 提出变分模型合并（Variational Model Merging），将现有模型合并方法视为高斯后验合并的特例。通过使用非高斯后验（如全高斯后验）推导新合并策略。理论证明更灵活的后验必然产生更好的Pareto前沿估计。在视觉和语言Transformer上验证了不同高斯族合并方法的效果。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，更好的高斯族（如全高斯后验）一致地产生更好或可比的Pareto前沿。与各向同性高斯后验相比，全高斯后验估计的前沿质量更优，验证了理论预测。在多种视觉和语言任务上性能提升明显，且计算成本可控。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文展示了贝叶斯思想在改进Pareto分析中的罕见应用，通过变分模型合并显著提升了多任务微调中的Pareto前沿估计质量。该工作为模型合并和贝叶斯优化提供了新视角，未来可探索更复杂的后验形式及扩展到其他多任务场景。

title: Mamba-FSCIL: Dynamic Adaptation with Selective State Space Model for Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
abstract: Few-shot class-incremental learning (FSCIL) aims to incrementally learn novel classes from limited examples while preserving knowledge of previously learned classes. Existing methods face a critical dilemma: static architectures rely on a constant parameter space to learn from data that arrive sequentially, making them prone to overfitting to the current session, while dynamic architectures continually expand the parameter space, leading to increased complexity. In this study, we explore the potential of Selective State Space Models (SSMs) for FSCIL. Mamba leverages its input-dependent parameters to dynamically adjust its processing patterns and generate content-aware scan patterns without session-wise projector expansion. This enables it to configure distinct processing for base and novel classes, helping preserve existing knowledge while adapting to new ones. To leverage Mamba's potential for FSCIL, we design two key modules: First, we propose a dual selective SSM projector that generates input-conditioned state-space parameters from intermediate features for dynamic adaptation. The dual design structurally decouples base and novel-class processing, employing a frozen base branch to maintain stable base-class features and a dynamic incremental branch that adaptively learns distinctive feature shifts for novel classes. Second, we develop a class-sensitive selective scan mechanism to guide dynamic adaptation of the incremental branch. It reduces the disruption to base-class representations caused by training on novel data, and meanwhile, encourages the selective scan to perform in distinct patterns between base and novel classes. Extensive experiments on miniImageNet, CIFAR-100, and CUB-200 demonstrate that Mamba-FSCIL achieves state-of-the-art performance.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Mamba-FSCIL，利用选择性状态空间模型动态适应少样本类增量学习，无需扩展参数空间。
light_analysis.motivation: 少样本类增量学习（FSCIL）旨在从少量样本中增量学习新类别，同时保留旧知识。现有方法面临两难：静态架构容易过拟合当前会话，动态架构不断扩展参数空间导致复杂度增加。因此，需要一种既能动态适应又不增加复杂性的方法。
light_analysis.method: 提出基于Mamba（选择性SSM）的FSCIL框架，设计双选择性SSM投影器，包含冻结基类分支和动态增量分支，分别处理基类和新类特征。同时开发类敏感选择性扫描机制，引导增量分支动态适应，减少对新类训练时对基类表示的干扰，并鼓励基类和新类的扫描模式不同。
light_analysis.result: 在miniImageNet、CIFAR-100和CUB-200数据集上进行大量实验，Mamba-FSCIL达到了最先进的性能。具体性能指标摘要未明确说明，但表明优于现有方法。
light_analysis.conclusion: Mamba-FSCIL通过动态适应而不扩展参数空间，有效解决了FSCIL中的稳定性-可塑性困境。其双分支设计和类敏感扫描机制为增量学习提供了新范式。未来可探索更复杂的SSM架构或扩展到其他增量学习场景。

title: Invariant Graph Representations for Continuous-Time Dynamic Graphs Under Distribution Shifts
abstract: Continuous-Time Dynamic Graphs (CTDGs) enable fine-grained modeling of evolving relational systems. However, most existing CTDG representation learning methods are tailored to in-distribution settings and exhibit limited robustness under out-of-distribution (OOD) shifts. Although recent causal approaches learn invariant representations via interventions, they are primarily designed for static or discrete-time graphs and become computationally prohibitive for CTDGs due to the combinatorial explosion of structural and temporal variations. To address these challenges, we propose CIR, a framework grounded in a novel structural causal model termed the ICCM. To avoid exhaustive interventions, we leverage the Normalized Weighted Geometric Mean (NWGM) to efficiently approximate interventional predictions. We further instantiate ICCM within a practical deep learning architecture that jointly captures invariant structural and temporal patterns through dedicated subgraph extractors, and maintains an environment memory bank to model distributional shifts across evolving contexts. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CIR consistently outperforms existing methods under diverse OOD scenarios.

light_analysis.overview: 提出CIR框架，基于结构因果模型ICCM，实现连续时态动态图在分布偏移下的不变表示学习。
light_analysis.motivation: 连续时态动态图（CTDG）能细粒度建模演化关系系统，但现有方法主要针对分布内设置，在分布外（OOD）偏移下鲁棒性不足。最近的因果方法通过干预学习不变表示，但主要针对静态或离散时态图，由于结构与时态变化的组合爆炸，在CTDG上计算不可行。因此，亟需一种高效且能应对CTDG中OOD偏移的不变表示学习方法。
light_analysis.method: 提出CIR框架，基于新颖的结构因果模型ICCM。为避免穷举干预，利用归一化加权几何平均（NWGM）高效近似干预预测。在实用深度学习架构中实例化ICCM：通过专用子图提取器联合捕捉不变结构与时态模式，并维护环境记忆库以建模演化上下文中的分布偏移。该方法无需显式枚举所有干预场景。
light_analysis.result: 在多种OOD场景下的大量实验表明，CIR持续优于现有方法。摘要未明确说明具体性能指标，但强调了一致性的性能优势。相较于基线方法，CIR在分布偏移下展现出更强的鲁棒性和泛化能力，验证了所提不变表示学习的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: CIR为CTDG在分布偏移下的学习提供了有效框架，通过因果视角和高效近似策略解决了计算瓶颈。学术上，拓展了动态图OOD泛化研究的边界；实际中，适用于需要鲁棒建模的演化系统（如社交网络、交通流量）。未来可探索更高效的环境记忆更新机制或细粒度子图发现策略。

title: Vikhr: The Family of Open-Source Instruction-Tuned Large Language Models for Russian
abstract: There has been a surge in the development of various Large Language Models (LLMs). However, text generation for languages other than English often faces significant challenges, including poor generation quality and reduced computational performance due to the disproportionate representation of tokens in the model's vocabulary. In this work, we address these issues by developing a pipeline for the adaptation of English-oriented pre-trained models to other languages and constructing efficient bilingual LLMs. Using this pipeline, we construct Vikhr, a series of bilingual open-source instruction-following LLMs designed specifically for the Russian language. ``Vikhr'' refers to the name of the Mistral LLM series and means a ``strong gust of wind.'' Unlike previous Russian-language models that typically rely on LoRA adapters on top of English-oriented models, sacrificing performance for lower training costs, Vikhr features an adapted tokenizer vocabulary and undergoes the continued pre-training and instruction tuning of all weights. This not only enhances the model's performance but also significantly improves its computational and contextual efficiency. We also expanded the instruction datasets and corpora for continued pre-training. The model weights, instruction sets, and code are publicly available.

light_analysis.overview: 开发了针对俄语的开源指令微调大语言模型系列Vikhr，通过重新适配tokenizer并全量继续预训练和指令微调，显著提升非英语语言模型的性能与计算效率。
light_analysis.motivation: 非英语语言（如俄语）的大语言模型常因词汇表中token分布不均导致生成质量差和计算性能低。现有俄语模型多采用LoRA适配器在英语预训练模型上微调，牺牲性能以降低训练成本。亟需一种更高效的pipeline构建双语LLM，以同时提升生成质量和计算效率。
light_analysis.method: 提出一套将英语预训练模型适配到其他语言的pipeline，核心包括：适配俄语tokenizer以平衡词汇表、在俄英语料上进行继续预训练、并对所有模型权重进行指令微调（而非仅使用LoRA）。同时扩展了继续预训练语料库和指令数据集。模型基于Mistral架构。
light_analysis.result: 与基于LoRA的俄语模型对比，Vikhr在性能、计算效率和上下文效率上均有显著提升。摘要未明确具体数值，但强调全参数微调优于参数高效方法，且公开了模型、数据和代码供复现与验证。
light_analysis.conclusion: Vikhr系列通过全参数微调和架构适配，为俄语LLM提供了高性能、高效率的开源方案。其贡献在于可复现的适配pipeline和公开资源，推动了非英语语言NLP的发展。未来可扩展至其他低资源语言或更大规模模型。

title: Evaluation Metrics as Averaged Outcomes of Fair Gambles
abstract: In the current practices of machine learning, the evaluation of forecasts has become a cornerstone of scientific progress. A multitude of evaluation metrics have been suggested and used to qualify "good" forecasts. What do those metrics share? How are they related? In this work, we use a protocol borrowed from game-theoretic probability to show that a large part of evaluation metrics can be viewed as averaged outcomes of fair gambles. Intuitively, a fair gambler is one which a forecaster would expect to fail. Hence, the gambler's ability to gain disproves the quality of the forecast. Standard evaluation metrics are then variants of choices of such fair gambles. In particular, this choice is structured along two dimensions, one of which separates calibration-type and regret-type metrics. In particular, this framework sheds light on the relationship of calibration and regret showing a theoretical equivalence in their ability to evaluate when being scaled appropriately, but the incomparability of obtained scores.

light_analysis.overview: 将评估指标解释为公平赌博的平均结果，统一理解校准与遗憾指标的关系。
light_analysis.motivation: 机器学习中涌现了大量评估指标来评价预测质量，但缺乏统一框架理解它们的共性和差异。现有方法未能揭示不同指标之间的内在联系，导致指标选择和解释存在困惑。本文旨在通过博弈概率协议，建立评估指标与公平赌博的对应关系，从而阐明校准型与遗憾型指标的本质区别与联系。
light_analysis.method: 采用博弈概率中的公平赌博协议：假设一个公平赌徒在给定预测下无法稳定获利，若赌徒获利则否定预测质量。将标准评估指标视为不同公平赌博选择的结果，沿两个维度结构化：一个维度区分校准型与遗憾型指标。该方法提供了统一视角，无需改变现有指标计算方式。
light_analysis.result: 框架表明校准型与遗憾型指标在适当缩放后理论等价，即它们评估预测质量的能力相同；但实际得分不可直接比较。这一结论解释了为何不同指标可能产生冲突评价，同时揭示了它们在区分好预测上的等价性。摘要未提供具体数值实验。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文提出评估指标的赌博理论解释，统一了校准与遗憾两类指标，证明了其理论等价性但实际得分不可比性。该工作加深了对评估指标本质的理解，为指标选择提供了理论指导。未来可探索将该框架扩展到更多类型评估指标或实际应用中的意义。

title: Understanding Deep Representation Learning via Layerwise Feature Compression and Discrimination
abstract: Over the past decade, deep learning has proven to be a highly effective tool for learning meaningful features from raw data. However, it remains an open question how deep networks perform hierarchical feature learning across layers. In this work, we attempt to unveil this mystery by investigating the structures of intermediate features. Motivated by our empirical findings that linear layers mimic the roles of deep layers in nonlinear networks for feature learning, we explore how deep linear networks transform input data into output by investigating the output (i.e., features) of each layer after training in the context of multi-class classification problems. Toward this goal, we first define metrics to measure within-class compression and between-class discrimination of intermediate features, respectively. Through theoretical analysis of these two metrics, we show that the evolution of features follows a simple and quantitative pattern from shallow to deep layers when the input data is nearly orthogonal and the network weights are minimum-norm, balanced, and approximate low-rank: Each layer of the linear network progressively compresses within-class features at a geometric rate and discriminates between-class features at a linear rate with respect to the number of layers that data have passed through. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first quantitative characterization of feature evolution in hierarchical representations of deep linear networks. Empirically, our extensive experiments not only validate our theoretical results numerically but also reveal a similar pattern in deep nonlinear networks which aligns well with recent empirical studies. Moreover, we demonstrate the practical implications of our results in transfer learning. Our code is available at https://github.com/Heimine/PNC_DLN.
comment: This paper has been accepted for publication in the Journal of Machine Learning Research

light_analysis.overview: 首次定量刻画深度线性网络中逐层特征压缩与判别模式。
light_analysis.motivation: 深度学习在特征学习方面表现优异，但层间层次化特征学习的机制仍不清楚。现有方法缺乏对中间特征结构的深入理解，尤其是线性层在非线性网络中模拟深层角色的作用未被揭示。该研究通过分析类内压缩和类间判别度量，探索特征演化规律，以解答这一开放问题。
light_analysis.method: 论文定义类内压缩和类间判别两个度量，通过理论分析证明：在近正交输入、最小范数平衡近似低秩权重下，线性网络每层以几何速率压缩类内特征，以线性速率判别类间特征。实验验证了线性网络的理论结果，并发现非线性网络呈现类似模式，在迁移学习中展示了实用价值。
light_analysis.result: 理论分析首次定量刻画了线性网络层次特征的演化模式。实验证实了理论结果，并揭示了非线性网络中相似的压缩和判别模式，与近期实证研究一致。此外，该结果在迁移学习任务中表现出实际应用潜力。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究首次定量表征深度线性网络中层间特征压缩与判别模式，为理解层级表示学习提供了新视角。实验验证支持理论，并扩展至非线性网络，同时展示了在迁移学习中的实用价值。未来可进一步探索更复杂网络架构下的特征演化规律。

title: Point-Voxel Absorbing Graph Representation Learning for Event Stream based Recognition
abstract: Sampled point and voxel methods are usually employed to downsample the dense events into sparse ones. After that, one popular way is to leverage a graph model which treats the sparse points/voxels as nodes and adopts graph neural networks (GNNs) to learn the representation of event data. Although good performance can be obtained, however, their results are still limited mainly due to two issues. (1) Existing event GNNs generally adopt the additional max (or mean) pooling layer to summarize all node embeddings into a single graph-level representation for the whole event data representation. However, this approach fails to capture the importance of graph nodes and also fails to be fully aware of the node representations. (2) Existing methods generally employ either a sparse point or voxel graph representation model which thus lacks consideration of the complementary between these two types of representation models. To address these issues, we propose a novel dual point-voxel absorbing graph representation learning for event stream data representation. To be specific, given the input event stream, we first transform it into the sparse event cloud and voxel grids and build dual absorbing graph models for them respectively. Then, we design a novel absorbing graph convolutional network (AGCN) for our dual absorbing graph representation and learning. The key aspect of the proposed AGCN is its ability to effectively capture the importance of nodes and thus be fully aware of node representations in summarizing all node representations through the introduced absorbing nodes. Extensive experiments on multiple event-based classification benchmark datasets fully validated the effectiveness of our framework.
comment: Accepted by International Conference on Robotics and Learning (ICRL) 2026

light_analysis.overview: 提出双点-体素吸收图表示学习，通过吸收图卷积网络同时利用点云和体素互补性并感知节点重要性。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有事件图神经网络通常采用最大或平均池化汇总节点嵌入为图级表示，无法捕捉节点重要性；同时现有方法仅使用点或体素单一表示，缺乏互补性考虑，导致性能受限。
light_analysis.method: 将事件流转换为稀疏事件云和体素网格，分别构建双吸收图模型，设计吸收图卷积网络（AGCN），通过引入吸收节点有效捕捉节点重要性，实现全感知节点表示汇总。
light_analysis.result: 在多个事件分类基准数据集上的广泛实验充分验证了框架的有效性，具体性能指标摘要未明确说明，但优于现有方法。
light_analysis.conclusion: 解决了节点重要性感知和表示互补性问题，为事件流识别提供了新思路，具有学术价值；未来可探索更多模态的互补或更高效的吸收机制。

title: EPMF: Efficient Perception-aware Multi-sensor Fusion for 3D Semantic Segmentation
abstract: We study multi-sensor fusion for 3D semantic segmentation that is important to scene understanding for many applications, such as autonomous driving and robotics. Existing fusion-based methods, however, may not achieve promising performance due to the vast difference between the two modalities. In this work, we investigate a collaborative fusion scheme called perception-aware multi-sensor fusion (PMF) to effectively exploit perceptual information from two modalities, namely, appearance information from RGB images and spatio-depth information from point clouds. To this end, we project point clouds to the camera coordinate using perspective projection, and process both inputs from LiDAR and cameras in 2D space while preventing the information loss of RGB images. Then, we propose a two-stream network to extract features from the two modalities, separately. The extracted features are fused by effective residual-based fusion modules. Moreover, we introduce additional perception-aware losses to measure the perceptual difference between the two modalities. Last, we propose an improved version of PMF, i.e., EPMF, which is more efficient and effective by optimizing data pre-processing and network architecture under perspective projection. Specifically, we propose cross-modal alignment and cropping to obtain tight inputs and reduce unnecessary computational costs. We then explore more efficient contextual modules under perspective projection and fuse the LiDAR features into the camera stream to boost the performance of the two-stream network. Extensive experiments on benchmark data sets show the superiority of our method. For example, on nuScenes test set, our EPMF outperforms the state-of-the-art method, i.e., RangeFormer, by 0.9% in mIoU. Our source code is available at https://github.com/ICEORY/PMF.
comment: 16 pages, 12 figures, 14 tables, IEEE TPAMI 2024, extended version of the ICCV2021 paper

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种高效的感知感知多传感器融合方法EPMF，通过优化数据预处理和网络架构，在3D语义分割中实现更优性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 多传感器融合对于自动驾驶和机器人等应用中的3D语义分割至关重要，但现有融合方法因RGB图像和点云两种模态的巨大差异而效果不佳。本文旨在有效利用两种模态的感知信息，即RGB的外观信息和点云的空间深度信息，解决融合性能瓶颈。
light_analysis.method: 提出PMF框架，通过透视投影将点云映射到相机坐标，在2D空间处理LiDAR和相机输入，避免RGB信息损失。构建双流网络分别提取特征，并用残差融合模块融合。引入感知损失衡量模态差异。改进版EPMF通过跨模态对齐和裁剪获得紧凑输入，减少计算开销，并探索更高效的上下文模块，将LiDAR特征融入相机流以提升性能。
light_analysis.result: 在nuScenes测试集上，EPMF的mIoU比最先进的RangeFormer高0.9%。其他基准数据集上的大量实验也证明了该方法的优越性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文提出的EPMF通过优化多传感器融合策略，有效提升了3D语义分割的精度和效率。该方法具有重要的学术和实用价值，可用于自动驾驶等场景。未来可进一步探索其他模态或更轻量的架构。
